description_long: This triumphal arch is constructed from large, rectangular stone blocks, giving it a robust and ancient appearance. The structure features two prominent cylindrical columns supporting a triangular pediment, with a single, wide arched opening at its center. The stonework is weathered, with visible variations in color from light beige to darker brown, suggesting age and exposure to the elements. The pediment is partially damaged, with a missing section on one side, adding to its historical character. The base is solid and rectangular, providing stability to the entire structure. There are no visible decorative reliefs or inscriptions, and the overall design is simple yet monumental, reminiscent of Roman architectural styles.
description: A weathered stone triumphal arch with two cylindrical columns, a single arched opening, and a partially damaged triangular pediment. The structure is robust, ancient, and visually striking with varied stone coloration.
description_view_0: A narrow side view showing the thickness of the stone structure and the depth of the archway.
description_view_1: A rear view with the archway in silhouette, highlighting the solid columns and the triangular pediment's outline.
description_view_2: The opposite narrow side view, again emphasizing the depth and block construction of the arch.
description_view_3: The front view displaying the full architectural details: two columns, a wide arch, and the partially damaged pediment.
